ddls:
CREATE TABLE "documents" (
  "docid" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "groupcode" INTEGER /* Group code categorizing the document */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the document */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the document */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the document (e.g., PDF, Word document, image) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief summary of the document's content */,
  "category" TEXT /* Optional category for further classification */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Keywords or phrases associated with the document */,
  "revision_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the document */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the document was uploaded */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the document was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("docid")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction event */,
  "docid" INTEGER /* ID of the document being interacted with */,
  "uid" INTEGER /* ID of the user interacting with the document */,
  "interaction_time" REAL /* Duration of interaction in minutes */,
  "analysis_score" REAL /* Score assigned to the document based on the user's analysis */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., view, edit, download, analysis) */,
  "device_used" TEXT /* Type of device used for interaction (e.g., desktop, tablet, mobile) */,
  "browser_platform" TEXT /* Details about the browser or platform used for interaction */,
  "user_notes" TEXT /* Notes added by the user during interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_interactions_uid FOREIGN KEY ("uid") REFERENCES users ("uid"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_interactions_docid FOREIGN KEY ("docid") REFERENCES documents ("docid")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "uid" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user within the organization */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("uid"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "groups" (
  "groupcode" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each group of documents */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the document group */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what this group contains */,
  "parent_group" INTEGER /* Parent group for creating a hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("groupcode")
)

CREATE TABLE "document_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "docid" INTEGER /* ID of the document accessed */,
  "uid" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the document */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the document was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location from where the document was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_document_access_logs_uid FOREIGN KEY ("uid") REFERENCES users ("uid"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_document_access_logs_docid FOREIGN KEY ("docid") REFERENCES documents ("docid")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the score entry */,
  "docid" INTEGER /* ID of the document associated with the score */,
  "average_score" REAL /* Average score assigned to the document by users */,
  "interaction_count" INTEGER /* Total number of interactions recorded for the document */,
  "average_engagement_time" REAL /* Average time spent by users on the document */,
  "user_engagement_score" REAL /* Engagement score calculated based on user interactions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_scores_docid FOREIGN KEY ("docid") REFERENCES documents ("docid")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., clinician, researcher) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with this role */,
  "role_hierarchy" TEXT /* Hierarchy of roles to simplify permissions management */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
